recommended_paths: Do not close RR-AUTH-03 until account-user token issuance is fail-closed for account-scoped bearer tokens and authorization ID comparisons are made strict with regression coverage. The route-binding work itself is directionally sound.
exact_next_step: Record reviewer resolutions in the governing TODO using the machine-checkable resolution table or equivalent gate ledger, then extract the derived resolution packet and decide whether another bounded review pass is still required.

findings.title: Account-user token issuance can still create unbound bearer tokens
findings.rationale: The canonical rule says account-user bearer tokens are stamped with account_id at issue/stamp time, but TenantScopedAccessTokenService::stampAccountId silently returns when no account can be resolved. AccountAuthenticationService::login can pass null accountId when no current account exists and no access account resolves, yet still issues a token. Account routes reject missing account_id today, but the issuer remains fail-open and leaves a reusable unbound account-user token class available for any future or missed account-route binding.
findings.suggested_action: Make account-scoped AccountUser bearer token issuance require an explicit/current account_id and throw when absent. If tenant-only AccountUser tokens are intentional, split them into a separate issuer path with non-account abilities. Add fail-first tests for login/issueForAccountUser without account context and for missing account binding at issuance, not only after manual token mutation.

findings.title: Authorization account membership checks use loose ID comparison
findings.rationale: CheckUserAccess::checkUserAccess uses in_array without strict mode, and AccountUserAccessService::permissions uses a loose collection where comparison for account_id. The later token-account comparison uses hash_equals, but the earlier membership and live-permission selection gates still depend on coercive equality. Normal Mongo ObjectId fixtures make exploitation unlikely, but authorization boundaries should not accept type-coercive ID matching if malformed/imported role data or context values enter the path.
findings.suggested_action: Normalize account and tenant IDs to strings once and compare with strict equality or hash_equals throughout membership and permission filtering. Add regression fixtures with numeric-string-like or otherwise coercion-prone IDs to prove account access and permission selection stay fail-closed.
